subject: [jlayton:exportd-netlink 3/18] net/sunrpc/cache.c:1074 cache_release() error: dereferencing freed memory 'rq' (line 1070)

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer asked Jeff to identify the specific patch causing the conflict between nfsd-next and -fixes branches, suggesting that the linux-next merge should have caught this issue.
reviews.raw_body:
Can you identify the patch that is the same in both branches?
Typically, the linux-next merge will catch that kind of thing.


-- 
Chuck Lever

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the duplicate patch was present in nfsd-testing, not nfsd-next, and will rebase the series on top of a sane merge.
reviews.raw_body:
My mistake. The duplicate is in nfsd-testing, not nfsd-next:

nfsd-fixes has:

   17ad31b3a43b sunrpc: fix cache_request leak in cache_release

...and nfsd-testing has:

    0b88d5fcde16 sunrpc: fix cache_request leak in cache_release

Cheers,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the remaining cache-related patches in nfsd-testing do not apply without a specific fix, and since nfsd-testing is based on nfsd-next which does not yet have this fix, it will be included by the end of the week.
sentiment_signals: no clear signal from reviewer's comment
reviews.raw_body:
Unfortunately the remaining cache-related patches in nfsd-testing
do not apply without that fix, and nfsd-testing is based on
nfsd-next, which does not yet have the fix. It will get it by the
end of the week.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch is not actually a code change, but rather a discussion about an issue in the Linux kernel where dereferencing freed memory occurs in the cache_release() function of net/sunrpc/cache.c. The problem was introduced when merging the nfsd-next branch and can be resolved by rebasing the series on top of a sane merge.
